MIT.name: Massachusetts Institute of Technology
essay_criteria: Alignment with MIT mission (science & technology for society)
interview_questions: What makes you a strong candidate for MIT?
What is a problem in the world you want to solve, and how will an MIT education help you do it?
If you could create a new course at MIT, what would it be and why?
Tell me about a time you failed. How did you handle it, and what did you learn?
What is the most difficult thing you've ever ha to learn on your own?
Tell me about your project you worked on just because you were curious, not because it was for a grade
Tell me about time you had a conflict within a team. How did you resolve it?
What is one way you have contributed to your community (school, town, or family)
How do you handle it when you are the smartest person in the room? How do you handle it when you aren't

major_specific_notes.Computer Science: Look for algorithmic thinking, personal projects, open source contributions
major_specific_notes.Physics: Look for research experience, mathematical depth
major_specific_notes.Biology: Look for lab experience, curiosity about living systems
